candidates.title: Phi Invariance Trichotomy via ρ = λ/η Order Parameter
candidates.summary: The normalized weight decay ratio ρ = λ/η determines a three-regime phase diagram for dynamic WD utility. Regime I (ρ ≈ 0.5, standard AdamW): all budget-equivalent schedules are equivalent (0.25% CIFAR-10 spread, CONFIRMED). Regime II (ρ ~ 5): alignment-aware scheduling provides O(ρ) improvement. Regime III (ρ >> 1): WD dominates. The 18.3× SGD/AdamW effect ratio is explained by SGD lacking AdamW's ℓ∞ implicit constraint. Formal Trichotomy Conjecture (T1) with proof sketch via Xie & Li (2024) + Defazio (2025) + Kuzborskij (2025).
hypotheses: H1: All budget-equivalent WD strategies produce accuracy within ±0.5% under standard AdamW (ρ = 0.5) — CONFIRMED, n=3
H2: SGD constant WD outperforms no_wd by ≥0.5% (confirmed: Δ=0.913%, d=12.17, p=0.0022, Holm-sig) — CONFIRMED
H3: SGD/AdamW ratio ≥ 10× (confirmed: 18.3× from verified disk data) — CONFIRMED
H4: Accuracy spread increases to 1-3% at λ=5e-3 (ρ=5, entering Regime II) — UNTESTED, P1-1
H6: BN ablation determines mechanism attribution (Path A vs Path B) — GATE 1
candidates.pilot_focus: Gate 1: BN ablation (mandatory before writing) + Gate 2: VGG-16-BN (architecture replication) + P1-1: λ regime sweep (central falsification)

evidence.swd_corrected: 90.710% ± 0.193% (n=3, seed_456=90.93% verified), Holm p=0.071 NOT significant

candidates.key_differentiators: More formal than Wang & Aitchison (2024) EMA heuristic; more dynamic than D'Angelo (2024) BN scale-invariance; explicit falsifiable boundary conditions; explains 18.3× ratio via mechanism (no ℓ∞ constraint in SGD)
risks: BN confound: invariance may require BN, not just AdamW (Gate 1 decides, Path A vs Path B)
Theorem provability: Adam saturation assumption A3 may not hold (Gate 0 check)
18.3× ratio CI may be wide (Bootstrap CI required in P0-1)
VGG-16-BN pilot hints no_wd > constant under AdamW — must confirm at n=3
candidates.next_action: Gate 0: theorem provability check (CPU); Gate 1: BN ablation (1 GPU hour); Gate 2: VGG-16-BN (6-8 GPU hours) — all before paper writing

candidates.title: ρ-Controller: Feedback-Stabilized WD via Gradient-to-Weight Ratio P-Control
candidates.summary: First formulation of WD selection as a closed-loop P-controller targeting ρ_t = ‖g_t‖/‖w_t‖ steady-state ρ*. λ_t = λ₀ × (ρ_t/ρ*)^{-α}. Computationally free. Lyapunov-backed faster convergence to ρ*. Only effective in Regime II/III (ρ>ρ₁); collapses in Regime I (standard AdamW). Primary secondary contribution replacing Super-Twisting SMC. Conditional on P1-1 confirming Regime II exists.
hypotheses: H5: ρ-Controller converges ρ_t to ρ* faster than constant WD by epoch 30 [UNTESTED - P1-2]
H5b: ρ-Controller achieves ≥0.3% improvement at λ=5e-3 (Regime II) [UNTESTED - P1-2]
candidates.pilot_focus: P1-2 (conditional on H4 confirmed by P1-1): ResNet-20 CIFAR-10, λ=5e-3, 3 seeds, constant vs rho-Controller α∈{0.25, 0.5, 1.0}
candidates.evidence: No pilot data. Literature confirms ρ_t dynamics (Defazio 2025). Innovator confirms no prior paper formulates WD as feedback controller targeting ρ*. Novelty: 8/10.
candidates.key_differentiators: Computationally free. Formal Lyapunov basis. One-line implementation. Does not require BN ablation results to be motivated. Formally novel (Innovator search confirmed).
risks: P1-1 may show Regime II does not exist (λ sweep flat)
ρ* estimation accuracy from Wang & Aitchison formula
May not exceed SWD in practice if rho* estimation is noisy
candidates.next_action: Conditional on H4 (P1-1 confirms Regime II exists at ρ=5)

candidates.title: Super-Twisting Sliding Mode WD as Chattering-Free Alignment Control
candidates.summary: CWD (ICLR 2026) is formally a relay sliding mode controller on the alignment surface. Binary switching amplifies noise when alignment hovers near zero. Super-twisting SMC provides a continuous control law with theoretical convergence guarantees without chattering. Conditioned on H7 (alignment signal informative in NoBN architectures). Demoted from primary secondary to conditional backup.
hypotheses: H7: Alignment signal is informative in NoBN architectures (std(δ̂_t) > 0.05 per layer) [UNTESTED - Gate 1 diagnostic]
H8: Super-twisting WD achieves ≥30% lower λ_t variance than binary CWD on NoBN ResNet-20 [UNTESTED]
candidates.pilot_focus: Gate 1 NoBN ablation first (verify alignment signal exists); then 15-min pilot of super-twisting vs CWD on NoBN ResNet-20 CIFAR-10
candidates.evidence: CWD paper shows random mask significantly degrades performance (loss 2.82 vs 2.56), suggesting alignment structure is real. AIS identical for CWD and random_mask in BN architectures (D1 confirmed). Contrarian's BN suppression concern validated by D1.
candidates.key_differentiators: First SMC-theoretic formalization of CWD; chattering-free alignment-aware WD; direct extension of ICLR 2026 paper
risks: Alignment signal may be structurally suppressed in BN architectures (AIS=0.416 for CWD = random_mask, D1 confirmed)
Stochastic discrete-time extension of super-twisting convergence guarantee needs verification
May perform similarly to CWD even in NoBN if chattering is not the bottleneck
candidates.next_action: Conditional on H7 being confirmed at Gate 1; otherwise demote to Appendix mention only

candidates.title: Gradient-to-Weight Ratio as Unifying WD Lens with Factorial Benchmark
candidates.summary: Merged into front-runner as mechanistic content for Regime I. r_t = ‖g_t‖/‖w_t‖ steady-state convergence (Defazio 2025) explains Phi Invariance. The 2×2×2 factorial design (BN/NoBN × AdamW/SGD × CIFAR/ImageNet) is retained as the empirical backbone. This candidate's independent novelty is superseded by Defazio (2025).
hypotheses: r_t trajectories for all 7 WD methods converge to same steady state under AdamW+BN (mechanistic explanation of H1)
r_t trajectories diverge under SGD (explains H2 WD-presence effect)
candidates.pilot_focus: Add r_t logging to existing training runs; plot trajectories (zero GPU cost)
candidates.evidence: Defazio (2025) establishes r_t steady-state convergence for normalized layers. Weight norm data confirms: AdamW 1.2% spread vs SGD 97% spread (T4, 81× ratio). Steady-state R_* = ρ connects Xie & Li and Defazio.
candidates.key_differentiators: Extends Defazio (2025) from LLMs to vision with factorial experimental design
risks: r_t steady-state convergence is Defazio's result; must credit properly
Overlap with front-runner — merge as mechanistic explanation for Regime I
candidates.next_action: Merge into front-runner mechanistic section. Retain factorial experimental design as empirical backbone.

candidates.title: Spectral Rank Feedback WD via Stable Rank Controller
candidates.summary: Dropped. In Regime I (standard AdamW), rank feedback provides no benefit. Only useful in Regime III where WD strategy already matters. Computational cost of SVD is prohibitive at scale. Demoted to Appendix mention only.
candidates.evidence: AlphaDecay (2025) covers the static per-epoch version. Closed-loop aspect retains 6/10 novelty but insufficient standalone contribution.
candidates.next_action: Dropped. Mention in Appendix if time allows.

synthesis_notes.consensus: All 6 agents converge on ρ = λ/η as the unifying quantity. Iteration 7 is a data-verified consolidation of Iteration 6. No new ideas needed — execute experimental gates.
critical_gating_experiments: Gate 1: BN ablation (1 GPU hour, 18 runs) — highest priority
Gate 2: VGG-16-BN full experiment (6-8 GPU hours) — architecture generalization
Gate 3: CIFAR-100 SGD no_wd seed_123 (20 min, 1 run) — complete n=3
P1-1: λ regime sweep (3 GPU hours, 36 runs) — central falsification
synthesis_notes.weighting_rationale: Theoretical and Innovator weighted most heavily (formal structure + ρ-Controller novelty); Empiricist shapes experimental design and provides verified data; Contrarian's BN concern elevated to Gate 1; Interdisciplinary's Maxwell relations retained as Section 2.5 physical interpretation; Pragmatist's factorial design adopted as empirical backbone.
critical_updates_iter7: SWD seed_456 = 90.93% verified directly from disk (iter_003/exp/results/sgd_baseline/cifar10/resnet20/swd/seed_456/summary.json)
Full Holm-Bonferroni table recomputed from verified disk data — only constant vs no_wd significant
cosine_schedule, half_lambda, random_mask, no_wd seeds 123/456 all confirmed present on disk
No new ideas generated — Iteration 7 is final data consolidation before gate experiments
Synthesis verdict: EXECUTE GATES, no further synthesis iterations needed
